Question:
                                What is the total number of goals and assists I have in the recreational indoor soccer league?

                                Question date:
                                2023/05/29 (Mon) 21:24

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                5

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET

- claim hot-path-8c3f66bd903e-claim-14 [ACTIVE] current_metric_value family=current_metric_value: The user had two assists in their indoor soccer league as of May 24, 2023.; scope=User's indoor soccer league performance as of the May 24, 2023 session.; context="The user mentioned the assist count while discussing indoor soccer with work colleagues."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:36a56982052f1c7dc918d4760cefb6c2 quote="I've had two assists in the league so far"
- claim hot-path-ec1d687c382a-claim-6 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user plays soccer.; scope=User's sports activities; context="The user said they were playing in a recreational indoor soccer league and later discussed soccer performance and nutrition."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:26a7e1e9b868a1e0a09d9b82b3d730b1 quote="I'm also playing in a recreational indoor soccer league"
- claim hot-path-8c3f66bd903e-claim-9 [ACTIVE] has_experience_with family=has_experience_with: The user has experience playing indoor soccer with colleagues from work.; scope=User's sports activities as stated in the May 24, 2023 session.; context="The user mentioned playing indoor soccer with colleagues from work while discussing running shoes and fitness."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:36a56982052f1c7dc918d4760cefb6c2 quote="I've also been playing indoor soccer with some colleagues from work"
- claim hot-path-ec1d687c382a-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user has a goal of fueling their body better for sports activities, especially for energy and recovery.; scope=User's sports nutrition goals; context="The goal was stated in a nutrition-advice conversation after the user's 5K charity run."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:26a7e1e9b868a1e0a09d9b82b3d730b1 quote="I'm trying to fuel my body better for my sports activities"
- claim hot-path-ec1d687c382a-claim-15 [ACTIVE] has_constraint family=has_constraint: For sports bar recommendations, the user wants bars that are high in protein, low in sugar, and taste good.; scope=User's criteria for sports bar recommendations; context="The criteria apply to sports bars used as convenient snacks during soccer and tennis games."; evidence=IMPORTED source=external:provided-text:26a7e1e9b868a1e0a09d9b82b3d730b1 quote="sports bars that are high in protein, low in sugar, and taste good"
- claim hot-path-8c3f66bd903e-claim-15 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user intends to incorporate stretching into their routine, especially after soccer games, to care for muscles and prevent injuries.; scope=User's post-soccer recovery routine goal from the May 24, 2023 session.; context="The user discussed post-soccer stretching and explicitly said they wanted to take care of muscles and prevent injuries."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:36a56982052f1c7dc918d4760cefb6c2 quote="I'll try to incorporate some of these stretches into my routine, especially after my soccer games"

LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/24 (Wed) 00:42
Transcript:
user: I'm looking to find a new pair of running shoes. I've been doing a lot of running lately, especially with the 5K charity run I participated in last month. Do you have any recommendations for good running shoes for someone who's been doing 3 runs a week with increasing distances? By the way, I've also been playing indoor soccer with some colleagues from work, and I've had several goals in the league so far.
assistant: Congratulations on your 5K charity run and your success in indoor soccer! That's awesome!...
...user: I think I'll try out the Brooks Ghost, I've heard great things about them. Do you have any tips on how to prevent shin splints, I've had some issues with them in the past, especially when I increased my running distance....
...* Run on a treadmill or indoor track if possible, as these surfaces can be easier on your shins than outdoor roads....
...user: I've heard that strengthening your core can also help with running form and reduce the risk of shin splints. Do you have any exercises or tips for improving core strength, maybe something that can be done at home or in a short amount of time?...
...1. **Plank**: Hold a plank position for 30-60 seconds, rest for 30 seconds, and repeat for 3-5 sets. This exercise targets your abs, obliques, and lower back....
...user: That's really helpful, thanks! I'll definitely start incorporating those exercises into my routine. By the way, I've been playing indoor soccer with my colleagues from work and I've had two assists in the league so far. Do you have any tips on how to improve my passing game in soccer?
assistant: That's great to hear! Congratulations on your assists, and I'm happy to help you improve your passing game in soccer!...
...**Additional tips for indoor soccer:**

1. **Use the walls**: Indoor soccer fields often have walls that can be used to your advantage. Practice using the walls to your benefit by passing the ball off them to create scoring opportunities.
2. **Play to the space**: In indoor soccer, there is less space to work with, so it's essential to play to the space and use your teammates to create opportunities.
3. **Keep it simple**: Indoor soccer is a fast-paced game, and sometimes the simplest passes are the most effective. Focus on making quick, short passes to maintain possession and create scoring chances.

By following these tips, you'll be able to improve your passing game and become a more effective player on the indoor soccer field!
user: Thanks for the tips! I'll definitely work on my passing game and try to incorporate some of those exercises into my practice. By the way, do you have any recommendations for good stretching exercises for after my soccer games? I want to make sure I'm taking care of my muscles and preventing injuries....
...3. **Focus on major muscle groups**: Target the major muscle groups used in soccer, such as your legs, hips, and lower back....
...user: I think I'll try to incorporate some of these stretches into my routine, especially after my soccer games. It's always good to take care of my muscles and prevent injuries. By the way, do you have any recommendations for good ways to track my fitness progress and stay motivated? I've been using a fitness app to track my workouts and progress, but I'm always looking for new ways to stay motivated and engaged.
assistant: Tracking your fitness progress and staying motivated are crucial to achieving your goals! I'm happy to help you with that....
...2. **Mobile apps**: There are many fitness apps available that allow you to track your workouts, monitor your progress, and set goals. Some popular options include MyFitnessPal, Strava, and Nike Training Club....
...1. **Set specific, achievable goals**: Setting specific goals can help you stay motivated and focused. Break down larger goals into smaller, manageable objectives....
- source external:provided-text:26a7e1e9b868a1e0a09d9b82b3d730b1 [external_record ref=longmemeval:4adc0475:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2023/05/23 (Tue) 10:18
Transcript:
user: I'm looking for some nutrition advice. I'm trying to fuel my body better for my sports activities, especially after that 5K charity run I did on February 20th. I carb-loaded with spaghetti the day before, which seemed to help. What are some other foods that can help me with energy and recovery?...
...5. **Dried Fruits**: Dates, apricots, and prunes are natural sources of simple sugars and potassium....
...2. **Electrolyte Balance**: Include electrolyte-rich foods like bananas (potassium), dates (potassium), and coconut water (sodium, potassium) in your diet....
...user: I'm also playing in a recreational indoor soccer league, and I've scored 3 goals so far. Do you have any nutrition tips specifically for soccer players?
assistant: Congratulations on your goals! As a soccer player, you require a balanced diet that provides energy, supports recovery, and optimizes performance. Here are some nutrition tips specifically for soccer players:...
...* Fresh or dried fruits (e.g., bananas, dates, apricots)...
...* Dates (potassium)...
...5. **Consult a Sports Dietitian**: For personalized nutrition guidance, consider consulting a sports dietitian who specializes in soccer or team sports.

By following these tips, you'll be well-equipped to perform at your best on the soccer field. Keep scoring those goals!
user: I've also been working on my passing game in soccer, which has improved significantly over the past few weeks. Do you have any tips on how to maintain my endurance and energy levels during a game?
assistant: Excellent work on improving your passing game! Maintaining endurance and energy levels during a soccer game is crucial for optimal performance. Here are some tips to help you sustain your energy throughout the game:...
...user: I've also been playing tennis with my neighbor every Sunday morning, and I've been working on my backhand, which has always been a weak spot for me. Do you have any nutrition tips that can help with tennis performance and recovery?...
...* Dates (potassium)...
...user: I'm thinking of trying out some new sports drinks to help with my hydration and electrolyte balance during my soccer and tennis games. Can you recommend some popular sports drinks that are effective and taste good?...
...user: I'm also considering trying out some sports bars as a convenient snack during my soccer and tennis games. Do you have any recommendations for sports bars that are high in protein, low in sugar, and taste good?...

            Retrieved entities:
            - entity entity:<0> [CONCEPT]: Indoor soccer; Concept named Indoor soccer.
- entity entity:<1> [CONCEPT]: Soccer; Concept named Soccer.
- entity entity:<2> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

hypothesis.description: Short answer that can be produced from the retrieved memory context.

reason.description: One short sentence explaining the judgement.

supported.description: Whether retrieved memory is sufficient to support the expected answer.

systemPrompts:
You are an objective evaluator for a long-term-memory benchmark.
Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
Return only the configured JSON object.
